{"key_event":"Coalition federalization and expanding civic mobilization","reasoning":"Coalition politics, regional parties, Panchayati Raj, media expansion, PIL/RTI activism, and identity-based mobilization made organized society more capable of constraining and redirecting the state, though communal violence and anti-terror laws limited civil-liberties gains. State capacity remained strong through nuclearization, Kargil, coercive security laws, regulators, and local institutions, but central executive autonomy, neutrality, and monopoly of violence were weakened by coalition dependence, riots, and insurgencies.","society_change":0.4,"society_power":8.7,"state_change":-0.2,"state_power":8.4}
